name: Fresh Corn Ice Cream with Almond Lace Cookie Fan
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: COOKING RIGHT SHOW #CR9737
Scrape corn from cobs, reserve cob lengths.
Combine corn and 1 cup half-and-half in a blender or food processor. Process in short bursts to finely chop the corn.
Add corn mixture to a saucepan with remaining cream, reserved corn cobs, and vanilla bean. Bring to a simmer over moderate heat.
While heating corn mixture, beat egg yolks, sugar, honey, zest, and chile in a bowl until well combined. Remove and discard cobs from corn-cream mixture. Whisk the mixture into egg yolk mixture slowly.
Discard vanilla bean seeds and return the custard to the pan. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until it just begins to thicken (approximately 180 degrees). Do not boil; eggs may curdle.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve, chill, then freeze in an ice cream freezer according to manufacturer's directions. Yield: 1 quart. Serve in chilled bowls with mint sprigs and almond lace cookies if desired.
Almond Lace Cookies:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Lightly butter and flour at least two baking sheets; refrigerate for at least 10 minutes.
Combine ground almonds, sugar, softened butter, flour, milk, and zest in a large sauté pan. Stir over low heat with a wooden spoon until the butter melts and ingredients are well mixed.
Place heaping teaspoons of batter about 5 inches apart on prepared sheets (batter will spread during baking). Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until evenly browned; turn sheets halfway through baking.
Remove from oven using a wide spatula. Shape cookies into tightly rolled cigarettes, or allow to cool and serve flat.
Yield: 16-20 cookies. Store in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or freeze for up to a month.
